question_text: A green filter is placed over a white light source. When white light passes through the filter, which colours are removed and which colour is transmitted?

options.label: A
options.text: All colours except green are absorbed by the filter; only green light is transmitted.

options.label: B
options.text: Green light is absorbed and all other colours are transmitted.

options.label: C
options.text: The filter reflects green light and absorbs red and blue light.

options.label: D
options.text: Filters do not absorb light; they only scatter it in different directions.

explanation: A green filter transmits (allows to pass through) green light while absorbing all other colours of the visible spectrum. This is why the light emerging from a green filter appears green to our eyes.
